title: thread_rng() used for RSA key generation
description: In persona.rs:10, `rand::thread_rng()` is used as the RNG source for `RsaPrivateKey::new()`. On Linux this seeds from OsRng so it is currently safe, but rand 0.8's `thread_rng()` is documented as a CSPRNG-seeded convenience wrapper — it is NOT a guaranteed CSPRNG interface. If the process forks after seeding (e.g. a future daemonization path) or if rand internals change, the key generation RNG becomes insecure. The correct source for key material in Rust is `OsRng` directly, which calls getrandom() and is guaranteed CSPRNG at the API level. Fix: replace `rand::thread_rng()` with `rsa::rand_core::OsRng` (re-exported by the rsa crate) in the `generate_keypair()` call.
close_reason: P0 fixes: AtomicU64, UTF-8 path, OsRng, 410 cleanup, constant-time key compare, checked_mul, SSRF guard, sanitized URLs, persona exists check, UTF-8 safe truncate
